name: Zero_Trust_Privileged_Access_Management_Architect
description: Acts as a Principal Identity Security Architect and Lead Zero Trust Strategist to design highly rigorous, identity-first zero-trust architectures for enterprise Privileged Access Management (PAM) and just-in-time (JIT) access.

enterprise_environment.description: Detailed description of the enterprise infrastructure, including cloud providers, on-prem legacy systems, and critical assets.

compliance_requirements.description: Specific regulatory or compliance mandates (e.g., SOC2, PCI-DSS, HIPAA) that the PAM architecture must satisfy.

name: Identity_Threat_Detection_and_Response_Architect
description: Acts as a Principal Security Architect to design highly rigorous Identity Threat Detection and Response (ITDR) frameworks to monitor, detect, and neutralize identity-based attacks.

identity_infrastructure.description: Detailed description of the identity infrastructure, including Identity Providers (IdPs), directories, federated access systems, and MFA mechanisms.

attack_surface_concerns.description: Specific attack surface concerns or recent incidents involving identity compromises, such as pass-the-cookie, MFA fatigue, or golden SAML attacks.

name: Non-Human_Identity_Lifecycle_Architect
description: Engineers robust zero-trust security architectures for managing the complete lifecycle of non-human identities (service accounts, API keys, OAuth tokens, secrets), addressing the specific complexities of highly automated, multi-cloud enterprise environments.

environment_topology.description: Detailed description of the deployment environment including cloud providers, Kubernetes clusters, CI/CD platforms, and existing secret management solutions (e.g., HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager).

operational_scale.description: The scale of automation, including the frequency of deployments, number of active service principles, and compliance/regulatory constraints related to access logging and rotation.

name: Threat_Intelligence_Fusion_Attribution_Architect
description: Synthesizes disparate Threat Intelligence (CTI) streams to mathematically attribute advanced cyber intrusions to specific nation-state or e-crime threat actors using Diamond Model and MITRE ATT&CK alignment.

intrusion_iocs.description: Raw Indicators of Compromise (IoCs) and behavioral artifacts recovered from the incident (e.g., C2 IPs, YARA rule hits, specific malware hashes, unique mutexes).

victimology.description: Specifics of the targeted organization, including industry vertical, geographic footprint, and high-value data accessed or targeted (e.g., European aerospace engineering schematics).

infrastructure_analysis.description: External analysis of the adversary infrastructure, including registrar details, ASN, TLS certificate pivots, and historical passive DNS records.

name: Advanced_SOAR_Playbook_Engineering_Architect
description: Formulates precise, highly complex, and automated Security Orchestration, Automation, and Response (SOAR) playbooks for resolving advanced security incidents while minimizing Mean Time to Respond (MTTR).

incident_type.description: The specific category of the security incident (e.g., Ransomware Outbreak, Cloud Data Exfiltration, Insider Threat Anomaly, APT Lateral Movement).

environment_stack.description: Details of the technical environment, including SIEM, EDR, Firewall, IAM providers, and specific SOAR platform in use (e.g., Splunk SOAR, Cortex XSOAR, Azure Sentinel).

operational_constraints.description: Restrictions on automation limits, such as requiring human-in-the-loop (HITL) approvals for destructive actions (e.g., isolating core business servers) vs. fully autonomous containment.

name: APT_Threat_Hunting_Hypothesis_Generation_Architect
description: Acts as a Cybersecurity Genesis Architect to engineer rigorous, intelligence-driven threat hunting hypotheses for proactive detection of Advanced Persistent Threats (APTs) using cyber threat intelligence and MITRE ATT&CK mappings.

threat_actor.description: The specific APT group or threat actor profile to target (e.g., APT29, Lazarus Group, FIN7).

target_environment.description: The technical environment or specific infrastructure being defended (e.g., Active Directory, Azure AD, EKS Clusters, macOS Endpoints).

intelligence_feed_summary.description: Recent threat intelligence data, TTPs, or IOCs to base the hypothesis on.

name: Firmware_and_UEFI_Bootkit_Forensics_Analyst
description: Conducts expert-level digital forensics on low-level firmware, UEFI interfaces, and persistent bootkits to uncover advanced persistent threats (APTs).

suspicious_artifact.description: Provide details of the extracted binary, memory dump, SPI flash read, or anomalous behavior observed during the boot process (e.g., modified DXE drivers, unknown PE32+ sections, compromised SEC phase).

environment_context.description: Target architecture details (e.g., Intel ME/CSME version, specific motherboard/chipset, BIOS vendor, secure boot status, TPM PCR values).

objective.description: State the specific goal (e.g., reverse engineer the payload, trace the persistence mechanism, analyze SPI flash integrity, assess bypasses of Secure Boot/Boot Guard).

name: macOS_ESF_Unified_Logging_Threat_Hunter
description: Formulates precise threat hunting queries and hypotheses targeting advanced macOS persistent threats using the Endpoint Security Framework (ESF) and Unified Logging.

threat_hypothesis.description: A high-level description of the suspected advanced macOS threat activity (e.g., in-memory payload execution, illicit consent grant via TCC, kernel extension manipulation).

logging_source.description: The primary telemetry source environment (e.g., native macOS Unified Logging, CrowdStrike Falcon on macOS, Jamf Protect, specific ESF event streams).

operational_constraints.description: Constraints regarding false positive tolerance, performance impact of queries, or specific SIEM query language to use (e.g., Splunk SPL, Elastic EQL).

name: Windows_ETW_Threat_Hunting_Architect
description: Formulates precise threat hunting queries and hypotheses targeting advanced Windows persistent threats using Event Tracing for Windows (ETW) and kernel telemetry.

threat_hypothesis.description: A high-level description of the suspected advanced Windows threat activity (e.g., direct syscall evasion, reflective DLL injection, kernel callback hijacking).

logging_source.description: The primary telemetry source environment (e.g., native ETW providers, Microsoft Defender for Endpoint, Sysmon, specific ETW event streams like Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-Process).

operational_constraints.description: Constraints regarding false positive tolerance, performance impact of queries, or specific SIEM query language to use (e.g., KQL for Sentinel/MDE, Splunk SPL).

name: Cloud_Identity_Fabric_Threat_Hunting_Architect
description: Architects advanced, high-fidelity threat hunting strategies targeting multi-cloud identity fabrics, focusing on anomalous lateral movement, federated trust abuse, and stealthy token exfiltration.

identity_provider_telemetry.description: Details regarding available identity telemetry, logging sinks (e.g., CloudTrail, Azure AD Sign-in logs, Okta System Log), and retention policies.

target_threat_actor_profile.description: Specific TTPs (Tactics, Techniques, and Procedures) of the targeted threat actor (e.g., APT29, Scattered Spider) regarding identity abuse.

multi_cloud_fabric_constraints.description: Information on the multi-cloud identity architecture (e.g., SAML/OIDC federations, conditional access policies, cross-tenant trusts).

name: Advanced_Sigma_Rule_Detection_Architect
description: Architects robust, highly-optimized, cross-platform Sigma detection rules tailored for uncovering complex evasion techniques and living-off-the-land (LotL) binaries.

threat_behavior.description: Highly specific description of the threat behavior, attacker technique, or evasion method to detect (e.g., 'Process hollowing via CreateProcessW and NtUnmapViewOfSection', 'Suspicious WMI event consumer creation').

targeted_log_sources.description: The primary event telemetry required (e.g., Sysmon Event ID 1, 8, 10; Windows Security 4688; AWS CloudTrail; Zeek HTTP logs).

environmental_tuning.description: Specific baseline noise or expected false positives to exclude (e.g., 'Ignore execution from C:\\Program Files\\AuthorizedApp\\', 'Exclude domain administrator accounts').

name: Living-off-the-Land_Behavioral_SIEM_Query_Architect
description: Architects advanced, high-fidelity SIEM behavioral queries targeting Living-off-the-Land (LotL) techniques, bypassing static indicator-based detections.

target_lotl_binary.description: The specific native OS binary being targeted for behavioral analysis (e.g., PowerShell, WMI, bitsadmin, certutil, bash, curl).

siem_platform_syntax.description: The target SIEM syntax required for the query output (e.g., Splunk SPL, KQL, Elastic EQL, CrowdStrike FQL).

baseline_noise_profile.description: Known legitimate environmental behaviors and administrative tasks that typically use the target binary, to be filtered out to reduce false positives.

name: Forensic_Super_Timeline_Analysis_Architect
description: Generates expert-level digital forensics and incident response (DFIR) super timeline analysis strategies, focusing on Plaso/log2timeline artifact correlation, pivot points, and anomaly detection.

intrusion_context.description: High-level overview of the incident, known compromised hosts, and suspected timeline of compromise (e.g., 'Suspected lateral movement via WMI on Oct 12th between 02:00 and 04:00 UTC').

artifacts_collected.description: The types of forensic artifacts available for timeline generation (e.g., 'MFT, USN Journal, Windows Event Logs, Registry Hives, Prefetch, Amcache').

specific_threat_indicators.description: Any known IoCs, threat actor behaviors, or specific anomalies to focus the timeline analysis around (e.g., 'Execution of unknown binaries in C:\PerfLogs, anomalous RDP logons').

name: Kubernetes_Container_Escape_Forensics_Analyst
description: Generates expert-level forensic analysis and response strategies for detecting, reconstructing, and eradicating Kubernetes container escape techniques and cluster-level privilege escalation.

kubernetes_audit_logs.description: Extracted Kubernetes API server audit logs detailing anomalous RBAC changes, pod creations, or exec sessions.

node_telemetry.description: Sysmon for Linux, Falco alerts, or eBPF-based syscall telemetry extracted from the underlying Kubernetes worker node.

container_manifests.description: YAML definitions of the suspect pods, including SecurityContext configurations, volume mounts, and capability drops/adds.

name: AWS_Lambda_Serverless_Persistence_Forensics_Analyst
description: Generates expert-level forensic analysis and response strategies for detecting, reconstructing, and eradicating serverless persistence mechanisms, malicious layer injection, and IAM role abuse in AWS Lambda environments.

cloudtrail_logs.description: Extracted AWS CloudTrail logs detailing anomalous UpdateFunctionCode, AddPermission, or UpdateFunctionConfiguration API calls.

lambda_telemetry.description: Amazon CloudWatch logs and AWS X-Ray traces capturing anomalous outbound network connections, unexpected child process execution, or prolonged execution durations.

function_configuration.description: JSON definitions of the suspect Lambda functions, including injected layers, modified environment variables, and attached execution roles.

name: OAuth_Illicit_Consent_Grant_Forensics_Analyst
description: Generates expert-level forensic analysis and response strategies for identifying and eradicating OAuth 2.0 illicit consent grant attacks and malicious application registrations within cloud environments.

audit_logs.description: Extracted cloud tenant audit logs (e.g., Azure AD/Entra ID Unified Audit Logs, Google Workspace OAuth logs) detailing application consent grants, role assignments, and token issuance.

application_manifests.description: JSON or YAML manifests of the suspect OAuth applications, including requested scopes, reply URLs (ACS URLs), publisher domain verification status, and credential keys/secrets.

post_compromise_telemetry.description: Mailbox rule creations, automated forwarding configurations, eDiscovery search logs, or anomalous Graph API queries originating from the suspect service principal/application.

name: deep_brain_stimulation_biophysical_architect
description: A Lead Computational Neurophysiologist agent designed to derive mathematically rigorous biophysical models of Deep Brain Stimulation (DBS) interacting with neural circuits.

stimulation_parameters.description: The properties of the extracellular electrical stimulation, including waveform shape, frequency, pulse width, and amplitude.

target_neural_circuit.description: The neuroanatomical target and the specific cellular populations involved (e.g., Subthalamic Nucleus (STN) to Globus Pallidus interna (GPi)).

electrode_geometry.description: The spatial configuration and biophysical properties of the stimulating electrode and surrounding tissue (e.g., anisotropic conductivity).

name: multi_modal_fmri_eeg_integration_architect
description: Designs advanced, mathematically rigorous multi-modal fMRI and EEG data integration pipelines adhering strictly to BIDS standards.

study_objectives.description: The core scientific or clinical objectives of the multi-modal study.

eeg_specs.description: Technical specifications of the EEG recording setup.

fmri_specs.description: Technical specifications of the fMRI acquisition sequence.

name: astrocytic_tripartite_synapse_architect
description: A Principal Computational Neuroscientist agent designed to analytically derive and simulate complex biophysical dynamics of the tripartite synapse, including astrocytic calcium signaling and gliotransmission.

synaptic_elements.description: The specific pre-synaptic, post-synaptic, and astrocytic components being modeled, including their spatial geometry.

signaling_pathways.description: A comprehensive list of astrocytic GPCRs, IP3 kinetics, calcium channels, and gliotransmitter release mechanisms.

stimulus_protocol.description: The precise experimental pre-synaptic firing pattern or exogenous neurotransmitter application protocol.

name: optogenetic_photocurrent_biophysical_modeler
description: A Principal Computational Neurophysiologist agent designed to analytically derive and simulate multi-state Markov models for optogenetic photocurrent kinetics and their integration into Hodgkin-Huxley membrane dynamics.

opsin_type.description: The specific optogenetic actuator being modeled (e.g., ChR2, NpHR, ArchT) and its biophysical variants.

light_stimulation_protocol.description: The precise optical stimulation parameters, including irradiance (mW/mm^2), wavelength (nm), pulse width, and frequency.

neuronal_geometry.description: The spatial distribution of the opsin expression (e.g., somatic vs. dendritic localization) and underlying host membrane properties.

name: basal_ganglia_td_learning_architect
description: A Principal Computational Neuroscientist designed to formulate biologically plausible Temporal Difference (TD) reinforcement learning models mapping onto the basal ganglia.

associative_learning_paradigm.description: The behavioral task or learning paradigm (e.g., Pavlovian conditioning, instrumental learning, multi-armed bandit).

basal_ganglia_circuitry_constraints.description: Specific neuroanatomical and synaptic assumptions (e.g., D1/D2 dichotomy, cortico-striatal connectivity, actor-critic mappings).

dopamine_rpe_dynamics.description: Parameters governing the dopaminergic reward prediction error (RPE) signal, including tonic baselines, phasic burst thresholds, and temporal discounting.

name: neurovascular_coupling_hemodynamic_response_architect
description: A Principal Computational Neuroscientist agent designed to rigorously construct and simulate biophysical models of neurovascular coupling and the Hemodynamic Response Function (HRF) adhering strictly to BIDS standards for multimodal fMRI integration.

neural_activity_profile.description: Specifies the spatio-temporal dynamics of the simulated local field potentials or synaptic activity driving the neurovascular response.

vascular_architecture.description: Details the structural and resistive properties of the local pial arteries, penetrating arterioles, and capillary bed geometries.

input_bids_metadata.description: <var>{{ input_bids_metadata }}</var> Contextual experimental metadata mapped explicitly to Brain Imaging Data Structure (BIDS) standards.

name: dendritic_cable_theory_computation_architect
description: A Principal Computational Neuroscientist to analytically derive and simulate complex dendritic cable theory equations and multi-compartmental synaptic integration models.

morphology_data.description: The dendritic tree morphology, specifying branching structure, diameters, and lengths.

biophysical_properties.description: The passive and active biophysical parameters, including axial resistance and membrane capacitance.

synaptic_input_protocol.description: The spatiotemporal pattern of synaptic conductances applied to the dendritic tree.

name: neuromorphic_spiking_network_biophysical_architect
description: A Principal Computational Neuroscientist designed to formulate mathematically rigorous, hardware-aware biophysical models of Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) for neuromorphic hardware, embedding precise membrane kinetics and synaptic dynamics.

neural_membrane_kinetics.description: The biophysical formulation and integration method for sub-threshold somatic compartment dynamics, including ionic conductances.

synaptic_plasticity_model.description: The specific, time-resolved synaptic weight update rules, incorporating temporal spike coincidences or complex dendritic calcium integration.

neuromorphic_hardware_constraints.description: The physical limitations of the target mixed-signal or digital neuromorphic substrate, such as weight precision, latency, and fan-in/fan-out restrictions.

name: neural_manifold_state_space_analyzer
description: A Principal Theoretical Neuroscientist agent designed to rigorously extract and analyze low-dimensional latent dynamics and topological structures from high-dimensional neural population recordings.

input_data_format.description: Specifies the format and structure of the high-dimensional neural population recordings (e.g., NWB, Neuropixels spike trains).

dimensionality_reduction_method.description: The specific manifold learning or dimensionality reduction technique to be employed (e.g., PCA, jPCA, LFADS, Isomap, TDA).

dynamical_system_constraints.description: The constraints or assumptions regarding the underlying autonomous or input-driven dynamical system governing the latent state.

name: fokker_planck_population_dynamics_architect
description: A Principal Theoretical Neuroscientist agent designed to analytically derive and numerically solve the Fokker-Planck equation for stochastic integrate-and-fire neural populations.

neuron_model.description: The single-neuron dynamics model (e.g., Leaky Integrate-and-Fire, Exponential Integrate-and-Fire) governing the population.

synaptic_input_statistics.description: The statistical properties of the incoming synaptic current, typically parameterized by the mean ($\mu$) and variance ($\sigma^2$) of a diffusion process.

boundary_conditions.description: The specific boundary conditions for the probability density function (e.g., absorbing boundary at threshold, reflecting boundary at resting potential, and reset conditions).

name: synaptic_plasticity_weight_update_architect
description: A Lead Computational Neuroscientist agent designed to mathematically formulate and analyze complex synaptic plasticity weight-update rules and learning dynamics.

plasticity_mechanism.description: The core plasticity mechanism to model (e.g., Spike-Timing-Dependent Plasticity, Bienenstock-Cooper-Munro rule, Oja's rule).

synaptic_variables.description: The biological variables mediating the synaptic update (e.g., pre/post-synaptic calcium concentration, NMDA receptor kinetics, generic eligibility traces).

network_context.description: The network architecture or local microcircuit environment in which the synapses are embedded (e.g., recurrent attractor network, feedforward cortical column).

name: Dynamic_Causal_Modeling_Architect
description: A Principal Computational Neuroscientist agent designed to formulate advanced Dynamic Causal Modeling (DCM) pipelines for effective connectivity inference.

imaging_modality.description: The neuroimaging modality used to acquire the data (e.g., fMRI, EEG, MEG).

neural_mass_model.description: The specific biophysical generative model representing the underlying neuronal dynamics (e.g., Jansen-Rit, canonical microcircuit, Taylor model).

experimental_design.description: The cognitive task paradigm or resting-state condition detailing the driving inputs and modulatory contexts.

name: graph_theoretical_connectome_analyzer
description: A Principal Computational Neuroscientist agent designed to synthesize and analyze whole-brain connectome data using advanced graph-theoretical metrics.

dataset_format.description: The format of the network neuroscience dataset.

node_definition.description: The parcellation or node definition strategy.

edge_weighting.description: The approach for calculating structural or functional edge weights.

name: hodgkin_huxley_biophysical_modeler
description: Generates highly rigorous, computationally sound biophysical models of single-neuron action potential dynamics using the Hodgkin-Huxley formalism.

ion_channels.description: Specific ion channels and conductances to include in the model.

stimulus_protocol.description: The current injection or synaptic stimulation protocol used to drive the neuron.

name: whole_brain_biophysical_network_simulator
description: A Lead Computational Neuroscientist agent designed to architect multi-scale, automated whole-brain network simulations integrating multi-modal fMRI/EEG pipelines (strictly adhering to BIDS standards) with fundamental cellular biophysics, including graph-theoretical connectome analysis.

multi_modal_bids_dataset.description: The structure and specifications of the BIDS-compliant neuroimaging dataset (e.g., resting-state fMRI, high-density EEG, and structural DTI/dMRI connectome).

cellular_dynamics_framework.description: The single-cell biophysical formulation defining the local neuronal population dynamics within each brain region.

large_scale_network_topology.description: The graph-theoretical structural connectome (derived from dMRI) dictating the anatomical coupling matrix between the defined neural masses.

name: continuous_attractor_neural_network_architect
description: Designs highly rigorous, computationally sound continuous attractor neural network (CANN) models for spatial navigation, memory, and cognitive representations.

dimensionality.description: The dimensionality of the attractor manifold (e.g., 1D for head direction cells, 2D for grid cells/place cells).

plasticity_rule.description: The synaptic plasticity learning rule governing the formation of the attractor topology (e.g., symmetric Hebbian, asymmetric STDP).

name: neural_field_theory_spatiotemporal_dynamics_architect
description: A Principal Theoretical Neuroscientist agent designed to analytically derive and numerically simulate high-dimensional spatiotemporal pattern formation using continuum Neural Field Theory.

neural_field_equations.description: The core integro-differential equations governing the macroscopic spatiotemporal evolution of population activity (e.g., Amari-Wilson-Cowan models).

spatial_connectivity_kernel.description: The explicit mathematical formulation of the lateral spatial connectivity function (e.g., Mexican-hat, exponentially decaying, oscillatory).

spatiotemporal_perturbation.description: The external stimulus, spatial boundary conditions, or parameter bifurcations applied to the neural field.

name: free_energy_principle_active_inference_architect
description: A Principal Theoretical Neuroscientist agent designed to formulate and analyze generative models, variational free energy, and active inference dynamics for perception and action under the Free Energy Principle.

generative_model.description: The structure of the generative model, detailing hidden states, observations, and precision parameters (e.g., POMDP formulation with A, B, C, D matrices, or continuous state-space models).

free_energy_functional.description: The specific formulation of the Variational Free Energy ($F$) and Expected Free Energy ($G$) relevant to the agent's task and state space.

belief_update_dynamics.description: The differential equations or discrete update rules governing the minimization of free energy, typically formulating perception as gradient descent on $F$ and action as sampling from beliefs over policies based on $G$.

name: biophysical_hodgkin_huxley_modeler
description: A Principal Computational Neuroscientist agent designed to analytically derive and simulate complex biophysical Hodgkin-Huxley action potential dynamics and ion channel kinetics.

cell_type.description: The specific neuronal phenotype or somatic geometry being modeled.

description: Formulates rigorous Open Economy Dynamic Stochastic General Equilibrium (DSGE) models incorporating international trade, exchange rate dynamics, and cross-border financial flows.

trade_structure.description: The nature of international trade (e.g., home bias in consumption, intermediate goods trade, pricing-to-market vs. producer currency pricing).

financial_markets.description: The structure of international financial markets (e.g., complete markets, incomplete markets with portfolio adjustment costs, local currency debt).

monetary_policy.description: The central bank's policy regime (e.g., independent inflation targeting, managed float, strict exchange rate peg).

exogenous_shocks.description: The external and internal structural shocks (e.g., foreign demand shock, terms of trade shock, risk premium shock).

name: financial_frictions_dsge_architect
description: Formulates mathematically rigorous Dynamic Stochastic General Equilibrium (DSGE) models with financial frictions, incorporating credit channels, financial intermediaries, and macroscopic risk.

intermediary_constraints.description: The nature of constraints on financial intermediaries (e.g., leverage constraints, agency problems, capital requirements).

firm_borrowing_frictions.description: Frictions faced by non-financial firms in borrowing (e.g., costly state verification, collateral constraints).

macroprudential_policy.description: The macroprudential or unconventional monetary policy rule applied by the central bank.

shock_processes.description: The structural shocks to the economy including financial and real sector shocks.

name: diamond_mortensen_pissarides_architect
description: Formulates mathematically rigorous Diamond-Mortensen-Pissarides (DMP) search and matching models to analyze equilibrium unemployment, wage bargaining, and labor market frictions.

matching_function.description: The specification of the aggregate matching function (e.g., Cobb-Douglas, CES) linking unemployed workers and vacant jobs to new hires.

wage_determination.description: The mechanism for wage determination (e.g., Nash bargaining, directed search, wage posting).

separation_rate.description: The nature of job destruction (e.g., exogenous separation rate, endogenous destruction due to idiosyncratic productivity shocks).

policy_intervention.description: A labor market policy or distortion to evaluate (e.g., unemployment insurance, employment protection legislation, minimum wage).

name: optimal_mechanism_design_architect
description: Formulates optimal mechanism design frameworks leveraging the revelation principle, incentive compatibility (IC), individual rationality (IR), and virtual valuation equations.

objective_function.description: The designer's objective function (e.g., revenue maximization, social welfare maximization).

agent_types.description: The distribution and characteristics of the agents' types (e.g., continuous type space, independent private values).

allocation_rule.description: The constraints or structure of the allocation rule.

payment_rule.description: The constraints or structure of the payment rule.

name: n_player_bayesian_nash_equilibrium_auction_architect
description: Formulates rigorous n-player Bayesian Nash Equilibria (BNE) models for auction theory, providing bidding strategies, expected utilities, and revenue equivalence analyses.

number_of_bidders.description: The number of risk-neutral bidders, typically denoted as $n$.

valuation_distribution.description: The distribution from which independent private values (IPV) or affiliated values are drawn (e.g., $U[0, 1]$, exponential).

auction_format.description: The specific rules of the auction (e.g., First-Price Sealed-Bid, Second-Price, All-Pay).

name: continuous_time_asset_pricing_architect
description: Formulates continuous-time asset pricing models utilizing Ito calculus and stochastic discount factors, providing fundamental PDEs for asset valuation and risk premium derivations.

underlying_dynamics.description: The stochastic differential equation (SDE) governing the underlying state variable or asset (e.g., Geometric Brownian Motion, mean-reverting Ornstein-Uhlenbeck).

investor_preferences.description: The utility function or stochastic discount factor specification (e.g., CRRA, Epstein-Zin, habits).

asset_claim.description: The specific cash flow or payoff structure being priced (e.g., European call option, long-term bond, equity dividend stream).

name: bayesian_vector_autoregression_architect
description: Formulates rigorous Bayesian Vector Autoregression (BVAR) models for macroeconomic forecasting and structural analysis, incorporating prior specification, posterior inference, and structural identification.

endogenous_variables.description: List of endogenous macroeconomic variables to be modeled (e.g., Log Real GDP, Inflation, Policy Rate).

prior_specification.description: The choice of Bayesian prior distributions for the VAR parameters (e.g., Minnesota prior, Normal-Wishart, Independent Normal-Wishart).

structural_identification.description: Strategy for identifying structural shocks from the reduced form (e.g., recursive Cholesky, sign restrictions, zero and sign restrictions).

forecast_horizon.description: The desired horizon for unconditional forecasting or impulse response analysis.

name: structural_vector_autoregression_architect
description: Formulates rigorous Structural Vector Autoregression (SVAR) models for macroeconomic shock identification, providing identification schemes, impulse response functions (IRFs), and variance decompositions.

endogenous_variables.description: List of endogenous macroeconomic variables (e.g., GDP growth, inflation, interest rate).

identification_scheme.description: The structural identification strategy (e.g., Cholesky decomposition, Blanchard-Quah long-run restrictions, sign restrictions).

exogenous_shocks.description: The structural shocks to be identified (e.g., monetary policy shock, technology shock).

name: mixed_frequency_dynamic_factor_nowcasting_architect
description: Formulates rigorous Mixed-Frequency Dynamic Factor Models (MF-DFM) for high-frequency macroeconomic nowcasting to handle ragged-edge data and state-space estimation.

observation_frequencies.description: The mix of data frequencies being integrated (e.g., daily financial variables, monthly industrial production, quarterly GDP).

factor_structure.description: The assumed structure of the unobserved latent factors driving the macroeconomy (e.g., single global factor, block-specific factors).

state_space_formulation.description: The specific state-space representation and assumptions regarding the idiosyncratic error dynamics (e.g., AR(1) idiosyncratic errors, exact versus approximate factor models).

estimation_methodology.description: The econometric approach for parameter estimation and latent state filtering (e.g., Kalman filter with Maximum Likelihood via EM algorithm, Bayesian Gibbs sampling).

name: staggered_difference_in_differences_architect
description: Formulates rigorous econometric identification strategies for panel data with staggered treatment timing, addressing heterogeneous treatment effects using modern DiD estimators.

data_structure.description: The nature of the panel data (e.g., balanced vs unbalanced panel, frequency of observations, N and T dimensions).

treatment_assignment.description: The mechanism of treatment timing and whether treatment is absorbing or reversible.

heterogeneity_concerns.description: Expected variations in treatment effects over time (dynamic effects) or across cohorts (cohort-specific effects).

identifying_assumptions.description: The specific parallel trends assumptions required (e.g., unconditional vs conditional on covariates).

name: local_polynomial_regression_discontinuity_architect
description: Formulates rigorous nonparametric and local polynomial Regression Discontinuity Design (RDD) estimators to identify local average treatment effects (LATE), accounting for optimal bandwidth selection and robust bias correction.

running_variable.description: The continuous assignment or running variable and the specific cutoff threshold determining treatment status.

treatment_fuzziness.description: Whether the design is sharp (deterministic assignment at the cutoff) or fuzzy (probabilistic assignment jump).

bandwidth_preferences.description: Preferences or constraints regarding bandwidth selection (e.g., Mean Squared Error (MSE) optimal, Coverage Error Rate (CER) optimal).

specification_concerns.description: Potential threats to identification, such as running variable manipulation (McCrary density test) or discontinuities in baseline covariates.

name: Synthetic_Control_Method_Architect
description: A highly rigorous prompt for designing and estimating Synthetic Control Method models in econometrics to evaluate policy interventions.

treatment_unit.description: The specific entity or region that received the policy intervention (e.g., 'California', 'West Germany').

donor_pool.description: A description of the untreated units available to form the synthetic control (e.g., '38 other US states that did not pass Proposition 99').

outcome_variable.description: The target variable to evaluate the treatment effect on (e.g., 'per capita cigarette sales', 'GDP per capita').

predictors.description: A list of pre-intervention characteristics used to match the treated unit (e.g., 'GDP, trade openness, schooling').

intervention_time.description: The exact time period when the intervention occurred (e.g., '1988', '1990').

name: dynamic_panel_gmm_architect
description: Formulates rigorous dynamic panel data estimators using Generalized Method of Moments (GMM), specifically Arellano-Bond (Difference GMM) and Blundell-Bond (System GMM), addressing endogeneity and panel bias.

dependent_variable.description: The primary endogenous dependent variable to be analyzed dynamically over time and across entities.

exogenous_regressors.description: Strictly exogenous control variables that do not correlate with past, present, or future error terms.

endogenous_regressors.description: Endogenous or predetermined variables (other than the lagged dependent variable) that require instrumentation.

gmm_estimator_type.description: The type of GMM estimator to formulate (e.g., Difference GMM / Arellano-Bond, System GMM / Blundell-Bond).

name: mirrleesian_optimal_income_tax_architect
description: Formulates rigorous Mirrleesian optimal nonlinear income tax models utilizing mechanism design and social welfare maximization frameworks.

agent_utility.description: The utility function representing agents' preferences over consumption and labor/income (e.g., quasi-linear in labor, fully non-separable).

skill_distribution.description: The continuous probability density function characterizing the exogenous distribution of skills/types across the population.

social_welfare_function.description: The social planner's objective function aggregating individual utilities (e.g., Utilitarian, Rawlsian, Generalized Bergson-Samuelson).

government_revenue_requirement.description: The exogenous revenue requirement the government must raise, balancing the aggregate resource constraint.

name: bayesian_hierarchical_model_architect
description: Acts as a Principal Statistician to design and formulate complex Bayesian hierarchical models with custom priors and MCMC sampling strategies.

inferential_goal.description: The inferential goal of the model.

prior_knowledge.description: Prior knowledge or assumptions for the model.

name: variational_inference_architect
description: Acts as a Principal Statistician to design and formulate complex Variational Inference (VI) approximations for scalable Bayesian analysis.

model_structure.description: The underlying Bayesian model structure (e.g., latent variable model, hierarchical model).

data_characteristics.description: Characteristics of the dataset, such as dimensionality, sparsity, and scale.

inference_objectives.description: Specific goals for the VI approximation (e.g., mean-field, structured, normalizing flows).

name: gaussian_process_regression_architect
description: Acts as a Principal Statistician to design highly robust, mathematically rigorous Gaussian Process Regression (GPR) methodologies for non-parametric Bayesian inference over continuous function spaces.

covariance_structure.description: The targeted kernel function representing the prior beliefs about the function's smoothness, periodicity, or stationarity.

likelihood_model.description: The observation model mapping the latent Gaussian process to the observed data, particularly emphasizing non-Gaussian or heteroscedastic noise.

computational_scaling.description: The strategy for approximating the inversion of the dense $N 	imes N$ covariance matrix for large-scale data.

name: dirichlet_process_mixture_architect
description: Acts as a Principal Statistician to formulate mathematically rigorous Nonparametric Bayesian models utilizing Dirichlet Process Mixture Models (DPMM) for cluster identification.

data_structure.description: The nature and dimensionality of the observational data to be clustered.

base_distribution.description: The choice of the base distribution $G_0$ for the mixture components.

mcmc_strategy.description: The specific Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) algorithm to be used (e.g., Gibbs sampling, slice sampling).

name: hamiltonian_monte_carlo_architect
description: Acts as a Principal Statistician to mathematically formulate and rigorously design Hamiltonian Monte Carlo (HMC) and No-U-Turn Sampler (NUTS) algorithms for high-dimensional Bayesian inference, optimizing symplectic integration and dynamic trajectory lengths across complex posterior geometries.

target_posterior.description: The unnormalized target log-posterior density function and associated parameter space topology.

kinetic_energy_metric.description: The kinetic energy function and mass matrix specification (e.g., Euclidean, Riemannian manifold) governing the auxiliary momentum variables.

numerical_integration.description: The symplectic integrator constraints, leapfrog step-size adaptation schemes, and dynamic trajectory termination criteria (e.g., NUTS criteria).

name: approximate_bayesian_computation_architect
description: Acts as a Principal Statistician to design and formulate mathematically rigorous Approximate Bayesian Computation (ABC) algorithms for likelihood-free inference.

data_generating_process.description: The structural definition and computational mechanism of the generative model.

summary_statistics.description: The set of chosen summary statistics for dimensionality reduction.

distance_metric.description: The mathematical distance metric for comparing simulated and observed data.

name: reversible_jump_mcmc_architect
description: Acts as a Principal Statistician to systematically design and formulate Reversible Jump Markov Chain Monte Carlo (RJMCMC) algorithms for trans-dimensional Bayesian model selection.

model_space.description: The set of candidate mathematical models, parameterizing their distinct dimensions and structural assumptions.

jump_proposals.description: The specific trans-dimensional moves (e.g., birth/death, split/merge) connecting the parameter spaces.

target_posterior.description: The overarching target distribution spanning the union of all model-specific parameter spaces.

name: double_machine_learning_architect
description: Acts as a Statistical Sciences Genesis Architect and Principal Statistician to mathematically formulate and rigorously execute Double/Debiased Machine Learning (DML) for causal inference, leveraging Neyman orthogonalization and sample splitting to estimate treatment effects in the presence of high-dimensional confounders.

causal_parameter.description: The target causal estimand (e.g., Average Treatment Effect (ATE), Local Average Treatment Effect (LATE), or partially linear regression coefficient).

nuisance_functions.description: The machine learning models and estimation strategies used for nuisance parameters (e.g., outcome regression, propensity score, instrument prediction).

structural_equations.description: The structural causal model (SCM) or underlying data generating process highlighting high-dimensional covariates and the exact treatment mechanism.

name: target_trial_emulation_architect
description: Acts as a Principal Statistician to design and formulate rigorous causal inference studies using the target trial emulation framework.

observational_data_structure.description: The structure and nature of the available observational data.

causal_question.description: The specific causal question to be answered.

name: Functional_Data_Analysis_Architect
description: Acts as a Principal Statistician to design robust nonparametric methodologies for infinite-dimensional functional data.

data_characteristics.description: The underlying characteristics of the functional data (e.g., discretely observed, noisy).

analytical_objective.description: The primary statistical objective (e.g., functional regression, curve alignment, principal component analysis).

computational_constraints.description: Any relevant computational or specific methodological constraints.

name: hidden_markov_model_architect
description: Acts as a Principal Statistician to derive maximum likelihood estimation architectures for continuous-density Hidden Markov Models using the Baum-Welch algorithm and EM methodology.

observation_sequence.description: The sequence of observed data vectors.

state_space_dimensionality.description: The number of hidden states in the Markov chain.

emission_distribution_type.description: The parametric family of the state-conditional emission probability distributions.

name: jump_diffusion_modeler
description: Acts as a Principal Statistician to rigorously formulate and solve parametric inference and simulation problems for Jump-Diffusion Stochastic Differential Equations.

drift_function.description: The parametric specification of the continuous drift component $\ mu(X_t, t)$.

diffusion_function.description: The parametric specification of the continuous volatility component $\sigma(X_t, t)$.

jump_intensity_measure.description: The specification of the Poisson jump intensity $\lambda$ and jump size distribution.

name: high_dimensional_sparse_regression_architect
description: Acts as a Statistical Sciences Genesis Architect to formulate rigorous, high-dimensional sparse regression models, specifically handling non-convex penalties (e.g., SCAD, MCP) via algorithmic optimization strategies like Coordinate Descent or ADMM.

penalty_function.description: The specific penalty function to employ (e.g., SCAD, MCP, Elastic Net).

optimization_algorithm.description: The numerical optimization strategy to resolve the penalized likelihood (e.g., Coordinate Descent, ADMM).

theoretical_properties.description: Theoretical assurances required, such as oracle properties or variable selection consistency.

name: joint_longitudinal_survival_architect
description: Acts as a Statistical Sciences Genesis Architect to formulate rigorous Joint Models for Longitudinal and Time-to-Event Data, specifically specifying the shared parameter framework linking mixed-effects submodels and hazard submodels.

longitudinal_submodel.description: The specification of the longitudinal mixed-effects submodel.

survival_submodel.description: The specification of the time-to-event survival submodel (e.g., Cox proportional hazards).

association_structure.description: The shared parameter formulation linking the two submodels (e.g., current value, slope, cumulative effect).

name: spatio_temporal_geostatistical_spde_inla_architect
description: Acts as a Statistical Sciences Genesis Architect to formulate rigorous Spatio-Temporal Geostatistical Models using Gaussian Processes and Stochastic Partial Differential Equations (SPDEs) approximated via Integrated Nested Laplace Approximations (INLA).

spatial_domain.description: The definition of the spatial domain and continuous spatial process properties.

temporal_dynamics.description: The specification of the temporal dynamics or auto-regressive structure in continuous or discrete time.

observation_process.description: The distribution and link function connecting the latent spatio-temporal field to the observations.

name: log_gaussian_cox_process_architect
description: Acts as a Principal Statistician to mathematically formulate and design advanced Log-Gaussian Cox Processes (LGCPs) for modeling complex spatial point patterns, including deriving intensity functions, specifying spatial covariance structures, and designing scalable inferential strategies via SPDE/INLA or MCMC.

spatial_domain.description: The definition and characteristics of the spatial or spatio-temporal domain under study.

point_pattern_data.description: The nature of the observed point pattern, including potential covariates and observation mechanisms.

inferential_objective.description: The primary goals of the inference, such as predicting latent spatial fields or estimating covariate effects.

name: multivariate_extreme_value_architect
description: Acts as a Principal Statistician to formally define, analyze, and estimate Multivariate Extreme Value Theory (MEVT) models.

multivariate_data_structure.description: The multi-dimensional data structure exhibiting complex tail dependencies.

tail_dependence_metric.description: The specific tail dependence metric or extreme value copula to model.

asymptotic_assumptions.description: Assumptions regarding the domain of attraction and asymptotic independence/dependence.

name: vine_copula_dependency_architect
description: Acts as a Principal Statistician to mathematically formulate and optimize high-dimensional Vine Copula models for complex, asymmetrical multivariate dependencies.

marginal_distributions.description: The specifications of the marginal distributions for each variable, including parametric families and estimation strategies.

dependence_structure.description: The hypothesized high-dimensional dependence structure, describing tail dependence, asymmetry, and domain-specific relational constraints.

graphical_model_type.description: The specific class of Vine Copula to be employed (e.g., C-vine, D-vine, or R-vine) and the criteria for structure selection.

name: causal_inference_dag_architect
description: Acts as a Principal Causal Inference Methodologist to design rigorous counterfactual frameworks and Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs) for observational data analysis.

research_question.description: The core causal question to be answered.

variables_list.description: A list of known variables, including exposures, outcomes, and potential confounders/colliders.

assumptions.description: Domain-specific assumptions regarding temporal ordering and unmeasured confounding.

name: asymptotic_distribution_mle_architect
description: Acts as a Statistical Sciences Genesis Architect and Principal Statistician to rigorously derive the asymptotic distribution of Maximum Likelihood Estimators (MLEs) and test statistics under non-standard conditions.

model_likelihood.description: The likelihood function or data generating process.

parameters.description: The parameter space and true parameter values, indicating if they lie on the boundary.

non_standard_conditions.description: Specific violations of standard regularity conditions (e.g., boundary of parameter space, non-differentiability, singular Fisher information).

name: empirical_process_theory_architect
description: Acts as a Principal Statistician to systematically derive weak convergence and asymptotic properties of empirical processes via functional central limit theorems.

stochastic_process.description: The underlying stochastic process or empirical measure to be analyzed.

function_class.description: The class of functions (e.g., Vapnik-Chervonenkis class) defining the index set for the empirical process.

asymptotic_goal.description: The specific asymptotic property to be established (e.g., weak convergence to a tight Gaussian process).

name: spatial_metapopulation_seir_epidemiology_architect
description: A Principal Disease Ecologist and Mathematical Epidemiologist framework to strictly model complex spatial meta-population pathogen transmission using coupled differential equations.

host_species_description.description: Detailed characteristics of the primary host population, including natural mortality and birth rates.

inter_patch_migration_network.description: The topology or mathematical structure of the migration/connectivity network between habitat patches (e.g., gravity model, adjacency matrix).

stochastic_forcing_conditions.description: Environmental or demographic stochasticity acting upon the transmission coefficient or carrying capacities.

name: stochastic_lotka_volterra_population_modeler
description: Rigorously models stochastic multispecies population dynamics using generalized Lotka-Volterra equations with environmental noise.

species_network.description: The adjacency matrix or interaction network describing the trophic interactions (predation, competition, mutualism) between species.

environmental_stochasticity.description: The mathematical form and parameters for the stochastic noise driving environmental variability.

spatial_configuration.description: The metapopulation structure or continuous spatial domain, including migration rates or diffusion coefficients.

name: Hands-On_Procedure_Coaching
description: Coach a trainee through a vascular access technique using a training model.

name: Objective_Skills_Assessment
description: Design an objective skills checklist for a surgical stapler deployment lab.

name: Simulated_Clinical_Scenario_Debrief
description: Provide constructive feedback after a simulated clinical scenario.

name: stochastic_multi_objective_optimization_architect
description: Acts as a Principal Operations Researcher designed to architect complex Multi-Objective Stochastic Optimization (MOSO) models. Formulates rigorous models dealing with uncertainty, conflicting objectives, chance constraints, and risk-adjusted Pareto frontiers.

objective_functions.description: A detailed description of the multiple, often conflicting, objectives to be optimized (e.g., maximizing expected profit while minimizing conditional value-at-risk).

decision_variables.description: The set of decision variables, including their continuous, integer, or binary nature, and multi-stage recourse actions if applicable.

uncertain_parameters.description: The stochastic elements of the model, including their probability distributions, correlation structures, or scenario tree definitions.

constraints.description: The physical, financial, or logical constraints bounding the system, specifically highlighting joint chance constraints or robust bounds.

name: pinn_stiff_pde_architect
description: Acts as a Principal Computational Mathematician designed to architect Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) for solving stiff non-linear partial differential equations (PDEs), focusing on loss landscape optimization and boundary condition enforcement.

governing_equation.description: The explicit non-linear, stiff PDE to be modeled (e.g., Allen-Cahn, viscous Burgers', or stiff Navier-Stokes).

boundary_and_initial_conditions.description: The exact spatial and temporal constraints, including Dirichlet, Neumann, or periodic boundary conditions.

stiffness_challenge.description: The primary source of numerical stiffness (e.g., singularly perturbed terms, multi-scale dynamics, or sharp boundary layers).

name: lattice_boltzmann_multiphase_architect
description: Acts as a Principal Computational Physicist designed to architect highly optimized Lattice Boltzmann Method (LBM) solvers for complex multiphase fluid dynamics, addressing interfacial dynamics and high density ratios.

flow_regime.description: The specific multiphase flow regime (e.g., immiscible droplet collisions, boiling heat transfer, or flow through porous media).

density_ratio.description: The kinematic and dynamic density and viscosity ratios between the fluid phases.

interfacial_challenges.description: Key numerical challenges at the fluid interface, such as spurious currents, high Weber/Reynolds numbers, or complex moving boundaries.

name: astrocytic_tripartite_synapse_calcium_dynamics_architect
description: A Lead Computational Neurophysiologist agent designed to derive mathematically rigorous biophysical models of astrocytic-neuronal tripartite synapses, including IP3-mediated calcium dynamics and gliotransmission.

astrocyte_geometry.description: The structural dimensions and compartmentalization of the astrocyte (e.g., soma, primary processes, fine perisynaptic astrocytic processes or PAPs).

neurotransmitter_pathways.description: The specific presynaptic neurotransmitter release mechanisms and corresponding astrocytic G-protein coupled receptor (GPCR) bindings.

gliotransmission_mechanism.description: The mode of astrocytic glutamate/ATP release and its feedback effect on presynaptic/postsynaptic neuronal targets.

name: metabolic_control_analysis_architect
description: A Principal Systems Biologist and Metabolic Engineer to formulate rigorous Metabolic Control Analysis (MCA) frameworks, computing flux control and elasticity coefficients using steady-state kinetic modeling and advanced differential equations.

metabolic_pathway.description: The complex biochemical pathway to be analyzed (e.g., Glycolysis, TCA cycle).

key_enzymes.description: The specific enzymes targeted for flux control and elasticity analysis.

steady_state_conditions.description: Environmental and cellular boundary conditions for the steady-state assumption.

name: non_stationary_13c_metabolic_flux_analysis_architect
description: Acts as a Principal Systems Biologist to rigorously formulate and solve Non-Stationary 13C Metabolic Flux Analysis (INST-13C-MFA) systems for determining intracellular fluxes from dynamic isotopic labeling data.

isotope_labeling_data.description: Dynamic time-series mass isotopomer distribution (MID) data acquired via LC-MS/MS or GC-MS.

carbon_atom_transitions.description: The defined atom transition network mapping carbon backbones through the specific biochemical pathways.

physiological_steady_state.description: The macroscopic physiological parameters defining the steady state (e.g., cell dry weight, specific growth rate, substrate uptake).

name: genome_scale_metabolic_flux_modeler
description: Acts as a Principal Systems Biologist to systematically formulate, analyze, and optimize Genome-Scale Metabolic Models (GEMs) using Flux Balance Analysis (FBA) and advanced constraint-based methods.

metabolic_network.description: The stoichiometric matrix or biochemical reaction network defining the cellular metabolism.

objective_function.description: The targeted biological objective for optimization (e.g., biomass maximization, target metabolite overproduction).

environmental_constraints.description: Nutrient availability, exchange reaction bounds, and thermodynamic constraints defining the simulation environment.

name: spatial_transcriptomics_cellular_communication_architect
description: Acts as a Principal Computational Biologist to rigorously model spatially-resolved ligand-receptor interactions, predicting cell-cell communication networks across complex tissue microenvironments using spatial transcriptomics data and graph-based mathematical frameworks.

spatial_count_matrix.description: The spot-by-gene or single-cell-by-gene count matrix (e.g., AnnData/h5ad format or Seurat Spatial object) containing spatially resolved transcriptomic data.

spatial_coordinates.description: The 2D or 3D spatial coordinate matrix (e.g., tissue coordinates in micrometers) mapping directly to the count matrix.

ligand_receptor_database.description: The reference database of known ligand-receptor pairs and protein complexes (e.g., CellPhoneDB, NicheNet, or a custom compiled list).

tissue_microenvironment_metadata.description: Metadata detailing tissue architecture, histopathological annotations, and identified cell types or spot deconvolution fractions.

name: single_cell_rna_seq_trajectory_inference_architect
description: Acts as a Principal Computational Biologist to computationally model single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) cellular trajectories and infer pseudotime dynamics using advanced dimensionality reduction and stochastic differential equations.

count_matrix.description: The raw or normalized single-cell RNA-seq count matrix (e.g., cell x gene matrix in sparse format or h5ad).

cellular_metadata.description: Associated metadata for the cells, such as experimental timepoints, spatial coordinates, or cluster annotations.

trajectory_topology.description: The expected underlying topology of the differentiation process (e.g., linear, bifurcating, multifurcating, or tree-structured).

velocity_data.description: Optional spliced vs unspliced count matrices to incorporate RNA velocity kinetics into the trajectory.

name: differential_alternative_splicing_isoform_architect
description: Architects highly rigorous, statistically robust bioinformatic pipelines for quantifying and modeling differential alternative splicing (AS) events and transcript isoform usage from bulk or single-cell RNA-seq data.

input_data_type.description: The nature of the RNA-seq dataset (e.g., bulk RNA-seq, scRNA-seq, long-read pacing/Iso-Seq).

experimental_design.description: Detailed description of the experimental conditions, replicates, and biological context (e.g., knockout vs wildtype, developmental timecourse).

reference_genome_annotation.description: Specific reference genome build (e.g., GRCh38, mm10) and transcript annotation file (e.g., GENCODE v43 GTF/GFF3).

modeling_objective.description: The primary objective of the analysis (e.g., identifying differential exon usage, calculating Percent Spliced In (PSI), estimating full-length isoform abundance, differential splicing network analysis).

name: metagenomic_assembly_taxonomic_binning_architect
description: Acts as a Principal Computational Biologist to architect scalable, high-resolution metagenomic assembly and taxonomic binning pipelines for complex environmental microbiomes.

environment_type.description: The environmental source of the microbiome (e.g., human gut, deep-sea hydrothermal vent, soil rhizosphere).

sequencing_technology.description: The sequencing platform and strategy used (e.g., Illumina NovaSeq short-read, PacBio HiFi long-read, Oxford Nanopore).

read_depth.description: The approximate sequencing depth or coverage expected per sample.

taxonomic_resolution_target.description: The desired level of taxonomic classification (e.g., strain-level, species-level).

name: chromatin_conformation_hic_contact_map_architect
description: Designs robust, mathematically rigorous analytical architectures for modeling 3D genome conformation and analyzing high-resolution Hi-C contact matrices.

resolution.description: The bin size or resolution of the Hi-C data (e.g., 5kb, 10kb, 1mb).

normalization_method.description: The mathematical normalization strategy to correct for experimental biases (e.g., ICE, KR).

structural_target.description: The primary genomic structural feature to identify (e.g., TADs, A/B compartments, chromatin loops).

name: gwas_polygenic_risk_score_architect
description: Acts as a Principal Statistical Geneticist to design robust, mathematically rigorous Polygenic Risk Score (PRS) predictive models integrating GWAS summary statistics and linkage disequilibrium architecture.

gwas_summary_statistics.description: The input Genome-Wide Association Study (GWAS) summary statistics including effect sizes, standard errors, and p-values (e.g., PLINK or VCF format).

linkage_disequilibrium_reference.description: The reference panel used for modeling Linkage Disequilibrium (LD) structure (e.g., 1000 Genomes Project).

target_phenotype.description: The complex trait or disease architecture being modeled (e.g., Type 2 Diabetes, Schizophrenia).

statistical_methodology.description: The algorithmic approach for PRS computation (e.g., LDpred2, PRS-CS, or Clumping and Thresholding).

name: epigenetic_methylation_hmm_architect
description: Acts as a Principal Epigeneticist and Lead Computational Biologist to probabilistically model DNA methylation states and identify differentially methylated regions (DMRs) using Hidden Markov Models (HMM).

bisulfite_sequencing_data.description: The raw or processed bisulfite sequencing data (e.g., FASTQ, BAM, or bedGraph format).

genomic_context.description: The specific genomic regions of interest (e.g., CpG islands, promoters, enhancers) and their coordinates.

hidden_states.description: The defined hidden states for the HMM (e.g., unmethylated, partially methylated, fully methylated).

emission_distribution.description: The probability distribution modeling the emission probabilities of observed methylation counts (e.g., Beta-Binomial).

name: crispr_cas9_off_target_predictive_modeler
description: Acts as a Principal Computational Geneticist to probabilistically model and predict CRISPR-Cas9 off-target cleavage sites using genomic context and thermodynamic parameters.

target_sequence.description: The primary 20nt sgRNA target sequence (5' to 3').

pam_sequence.description: The Protospacer Adjacent Motif (PAM) sequence (e.g., NGG).
